claim: Character-level tokenization with only 65 tokens covers all characters in Shakespeare text, making bigram combinations tractable (65 * 65 = 4,225 possible bigrams) and avoiding convergence issues
actionable: Use character-level tokenization for small-scale LLM training on limited data to ensure model convergence rather than attempting BPE tokenization
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Tokenizer choice is the most critical decision when creating a new transformer model, with profound implications for training time and quality
actionable: Spend significant time designing and iterating on your tokenizer before committing to model architecture or training
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: For small models with limited data, 256 block size is reasonable; larger context windows require architectural changes to maintain training stability
actionable: Don't simply increase block_size without adapting architecture—verify that training remains stable when scaling context length
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Residual connections prevent layers from completely reinventing themselves; each layer makes small changes rather than large transformations to maintain training stability
actionable: Implement residual connections (x = x + attention/mlp) rather than direct transformations in deep transformer layers
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Layer normalization prevents activation explosion by scaling down large values, allowing stable training through many layers
actionable: Apply layer normalization after transformations prone to large magnitude changes to maintain activation stability
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Transformers can be implemented in ~100 lines of code using PyTorch, making the core architecture accessible to implement from scratch
actionable: Start with a minimal transformer implementation in PyTorch to understand fundamentals before using high-level libraries
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Learning rate warm-up phase followed by cosine decay is standard practice: start low, peak at ~100 steps, then decay to 5000+ steps
actionable: Configure AdamW optimizer with 100-step warm-up phase followed by cosine annealing learning rate schedule for stable training
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Model loss decreases predictably with training stages: ~4.17 (random) → 3.3 (character freq) → 2.5 (bigrams) → 1.5 (words) → <1.0 (good performance but overfitting risk)
actionable: Monitor loss trajectory against these milestones to detect overfitting; stop training around loss=1.0-1.2 before validation loss increases
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Validation loss that increases while training loss decreases is the clearest signal of overfitting; stop training at that point
actionable: Track validation loss separately and stop training when it begins increasing, even if training loss continues decreasing
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Temperature sampling (e.g., 0.7) prevents models from using greedy decoding which makes outputs boring; enables selective use of lower-probability tokens
actionable: Use temperature=0.7 for text generation to balance coherence with creativity; avoid greedy decoding for LLM outputs
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Top-K sampling prevents the model from sampling extremely unlikely tokens by restricting to the K most probable tokens
actionable: Apply top-K sampling alongside temperature to prevent hallucinations from extremely low-probability tokens
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Using seeds in inference makes non-deterministic generation reproducible for evaluation and testing
actionable: Set random seed before inference to ensure reproducible outputs across runs for consistent evaluation
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Post-training and reasoning abilities come from training data quality and methods, not base architecture; Gemini 3 to 3.1 improvement was mainly better reasoning data
actionable: Invest heavily in post-training data curation and fine-tuning methods rather than architectural changes for performance gains
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Scale AI and similar labeling companies provide high-quality training data, and data quality directly impacts model performance more than architecture changes
actionable: Budget for professional data labeling services for post-training data rather than spending primarily on compute for training
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Different attention heads learn to attend to different features (e.g., punctuation, grammar); multiple heads provide redundancy and specialization
actionable: Use multiple attention heads (6+ for small models) to enable the model to learn diverse linguistic patterns in parallel
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally

claim: Multimodal models use separate encoders (video/audio) to produce embeddings that are injected into the transformer's embedding layer rather than native tokens
actionable: For multimodal inputs, use domain-specific encoders and inject their embeddings into standard embedding dimensions
source_title: Training an LLM from Scratch, Locally
